key_insight: A hybrid UNet-Transformer can learn to map composite microstructure images directly to stress and strain fields, providing a fast surrogate for FEM.
engineering_relevance: This enables rapid field prediction for composite design and screening, reducing reliance on expensive finite element simulations.
method: Input: geometry images of two-phase composite microstructures
Model: UNet encoder-decoder with Transformer-based global context
Output: 10 mechanical field types across multiple tessellations and boundary conditions
evidence_signal: The model reports very strong performance on most subdatasets, including peak values such as R2=0.9991 and SSIM=0.9936, with MAE below 0.05 on six of eight evaluated subdatasets.
watch_item: Accuracy drops on irregular square-grid cases with sparse soft-phase inclusions, indicating difficulty capturing sharp stress discontinuities.
paper_title: Hybrid Unet-Transformer Model for Generating Stress and Strain Fields from Composite Geometrics
